requirement: Contract Number
reasoning: This dimension is a key identifier for grouping data for submission to CMS.

requirement: Plan Benefit Package (PBP)
reasoning: This dimension is a key identifier for grouping data for submission to CMS.
This dimension defines the temporal scope for which data must be collected and submitted to CMS.

requirement: Data must be for Initial Determinations (Coverage Decisions, Payment Decisions) and Reconsiderations (Coverage Decisions, Payment Decisions) made in the reporting period.
reasoning: This is a critical filter defining which records are included in the data submission to CMS.

requirement: Completed organization determinations and reconsiderations (i.e., all required notification has occurred) during the reporting period, regardless of when the request was received.
reasoning: This specifies the status and timing criteria for inclusion in the data submission to CMS.

requirement: Organization determinations or reconsiderations where a substantive decision has been made and processed in accordance with 42 C.F.R. Part 422, Subpart M.
reasoning: This defines the type of decisions to be included, implying a specific status or outcome for data submission.

requirement: Denial of a Medicare request for coverage (payment or provision) of an item or service as either partially favorable or adverse, regardless of whether Medicaid payment or provision ultimately is provided.
reasoning: This clarifies how certain denial types should be reported for data submission, irrespective of other payment sources.

requirement: Dual Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNPs) that are applicable integrated plans should report a request for a Medicare item or service based on the outcome of applying both Medicare and Medicaid coverage criteria.
reasoning: This is a specific reporting rule for D-SNPs, affecting how their data is prepared for submission to CMS.

requirement: Denials based on exhaustion of Medicare benefits.
reasoning: This specifies a particular type of denial that must be included in the data submission.

requirement: Organization determinations that involve hospital discharges and transfers to post-acute care settings.
reasoning: This specifies a category of determinations to be included in the data submission.

requirement: Requests for supplemental services that meet the criteria defined in 42 CFR § 422.100(c)(2).
reasoning: This specifies a category of requests to be included based on regulatory criteria for data submission.

requirement: Decisions to extend or continue coverage of the same item or service as a separate, fully favorable organization determination.
reasoning: This clarifies how certain favorable decisions should be reported as separate determinations for data submission.

requirement: If an organization determination includes more than one service, include all multiple line items as separate entries with the same OD number.
reasoning: This is a data structuring rule for submission, ensuring granular reporting to CMS.

requirement: Requests submitted by staff of a physician’s office on behalf of the physician should be reported as contract or non-contract provider, as appropriate.
reasoning: This is a classification rule for the 'Requesting Party' field for data submission.

requirement: Provider’s NPI must be reported on a service decision when the request is submitted by (or on behalf of) a provider or when an enrollee submits a service request and identifies a provider had ordered the requested item, service, or Part B drug or the enrollee requested to receive the item, service, or Part B drug from a specific provider.
reasoning: This is a data quality rule specifying when the NPI field is mandatory for accurate reporting.

requirement: Data must be submitted at the contract number/PBP level.
reasoning: This defines the granularity and grouping for data submission to CMS.

requirement: Compliance with reporting requirements is a contractual obligation of all MA organizations.
reasoning: This states an ongoing contractual obligation, implying monitoring of overall compliance with reporting requirements.

requirement: Data must be accurate and submitted in a timely manner.
reasoning: This is a general compliance requirement for data quality and submission timeliness, requiring ongoing monitoring.

requirement: Data submissions are due by 11:59 p.m. Pacific Time on the date of the reporting deadline.
reasoning: This is a specific deadline that needs to be monitored for compliance.

requirement: Only data reflecting a good faith effort to provide accurate responses will count as timely submitted.
reasoning: This rule links timeliness to accuracy, implying a quality check on the submitted data.

requirement: CMS tracks resubmissions, including those made after the deadline.
reasoning: This indicates CMS's monitoring activity related to submission behavior, which is a compliance concern.

requirement: CMS expects data to be accurate on the date of submission.
reasoning: This is a direct statement about data accuracy, a core data quality principle.

requirement: Organizations must store revised data and follow record retention requirements.
reasoning: This is an ongoing compliance obligation related to data management and record keeping.

requirement: Organizations should report data based on their interpretation of the Service Level Data Collection and Technical Specifications and be able to support their decisions.
reasoning: This is an ongoing compliance obligation related to data interpretation and defensibility under audit.

requirement: Organizations are required to report complete quarters that precede non-renewal or termination of the plan (PBP) or contract (e.g., if H0000 terminates May 31, report Q1; if H0000 non-renews Dec 31, report all quarters).
reasoning: This specifies the scope of data to be submitted in specific organizational change scenarios, impacting data inclusion.

requirement: HPMS will not allow the entry of '>', '<', ';', or decimals in any data entry field or uploaded file.
reasoning: This is a specific data validation rule for input fields, ensuring data quality.

requirement: Organizations must not submit 'placeholder' data (e.g., '99999').
reasoning: This is a data quality rule against submitting invalid or non-meaningful data.

requirement: Numeric fields do not allow characters.
reasoning: This is a data type validation rule, ensuring data quality.

requirement: Where there are multiple options, a single selection must be made.
reasoning: This is a data validation rule for categorical fields, ensuring single-choice selection and data quality.

requirement: CMS generally does not grant extensions to reporting deadlines.
reasoning: This reinforces the strictness of compliance with deadlines, impacting monitoring.

requirement: Any assumptions made for timely data submission must be fully documented and defensible under audit.
reasoning: This is an ongoing compliance obligation related to data governance and auditability.

requirement: Resubmission requests are considered through the HPMS Plan Reporting Module (PRM).
reasoning: This describes a process related to compliance management and reporting.

requirement: Formal request to resubmit data is required once a reporting deadline has passed.
reasoning: This describes a process related to compliance management and reporting.

requirement: Approval for resubmission requests is for 7 days from CMS review and approval.
reasoning: This describes a process related to compliance management and reporting.

requirement: Organizations should not submit resubmission requests until data is available.
reasoning: This is a procedural rule to ensure meaningful data is submitted, thus a data quality concern.

requirement: Late data (submitted after deadline) may not be incorporated within CMS data analyses and reporting.
reasoning: This highlights the importance of timely submission for CMS's internal processes, making it a compliance concern.

requirement: HPMS will not allow the resubmission of data identical to the original submission.
reasoning: This is a data quality rule to ensure resubmissions are actual corrections and not redundant.

requirement: Submission of inaccurate or incorrect data does not satisfy the obligation to report.
reasoning: This is a fundamental statement about data accuracy, a core data quality principle.

requirement: Corrections are appropriate for errors made at the original submission date or as indicated by CMS.
reasoning: This describes the conditions under which corrections (resubmissions) are valid, related to compliance.

requirement: A plan can only initiate a resubmission request after the original reporting deadline has expired.
reasoning: This describes a procedural rule for compliance with resubmission processes.

requirement: If CMS revises technical specifications, plans must incorporate changes beginning the following reporting period from the publication date (e.g., March update affects Q2 data).
reasoning: This is an ongoing compliance obligation to adapt to updated specifications for reporting.

requirement: Validation checks should be performed by each organization prior to data submission (e.g., reconsideration 'date request received' >= organization determination 'date of decision').
reasoning: This explicitly states a requirement for pre-submission data validation, which is a data quality activity.

requirement: Adverse Decision means an item, service, or Part B drug was denied in whole.
reasoning: This provides a definition crucial for accurate classification and reporting of the 'Adverse' disposition, ensuring data quality.

requirement: Clean Claim is a claim with no defect, impropriety, lack of required documentation, or special circumstance preventing timely payment, conforming to Original Medicare clean claim requirements (42 CFR §422.500(b)).
reasoning: This provides a definition crucial for accurate classification and reporting of 'Clean Claim', ensuring data quality.

requirement: Fully Favorable decision means an item, service, or Part B drug was covered in whole.
reasoning: This provides a definition crucial for accurate classification and reporting of the 'Fully Favorable' disposition, ensuring data quality.

requirement: Internal Coverage Criteria refers to an MA organization’s established coverage criteria as defined in 42 CFR § 422.101(b)(6).
reasoning: This provides a definition crucial for accurate reporting of whether internal coverage criteria were applied, ensuring data quality.

requirement: Organization Determination includes auto-adjudicated claims, service authorizations (prior-approvals, concurrent authorization), requests to continue previously authorized ongoing courses of treatment, and reduction/discontinuation of covered services. It includes pre-service requests from enrollee, representative, contract/non-contract providers, and claims payment requests.
reasoning: This provides a comprehensive definition of what types of events fall under 'Organization Determination' for reporting, directly impacting data inclusion for submission.

requirement: Concurrent Authorization is a coverage decision made contemporaneously to when an enrollee is receiving the item/service/Part B drug, without prior authorization.
reasoning: This provides a definition crucial for accurate classification of determination types, ensuring data quality.

requirement: Partially Favorable decision means an item, service, or Part B drug was partially covered (e.g., some claim line items paid, or fewer services authorized than requested).
reasoning: This provides a definition crucial for accurate classification and reporting of the 'Partially Favorable' disposition, ensuring data quality.

requirement: Prior Approval refers to coverage decisions made before provision of item/service/Part B drug, including prior authorization requests and voluntary pre-service requests.
reasoning: This provides a definition crucial for accurate reporting related to prior approvals, ensuring data quality.

requirement: National PACE Plans
reasoning: This specifies an organizational type to be excluded from data submission to CMS.

requirement: 1833 Cost Plans
reasoning: This specifies an organizational type to be excluded from data submission to CMS.

requirement: Medicare-Medicaid Plans (MMPs)
reasoning: This specifies an organizational type to be excluded from data submission to CMS.

requirement: Independent Review Entity (IRE) decisions
reasoning: This specifies a type of decision to be excluded from data submission to CMS.

requirement: Reopenings requested or completed by the IRE, Administrative Law Judge (ALJ), or Appeals Council
reasoning: This specifies types of reopenings/reviews to be excluded from data submission to CMS.

requirement: Duplicate payment requests concerning the same service or item
reasoning: This specifies a condition for excluding duplicate requests from data submission to CMS.

requirement: Payment requests returned to a provider/supplier in which a substantive decision (fully favorable, partially favorable or adverse) has not been made (e.g., incomplete forms, clerical errors)
reasoning: This specifies a condition for excluding requests that haven't reached a substantive decision from data submission to CMS.

requirement: Part B drugs paid or denied at the pharmacy and point-of-sale Part B drug claim rejections (unless the plan subsequently processes an organization determination)
reasoning: This specifies types of Part B drug transactions to be excluded unless they become an OD for data submission to CMS.

requirement: Quality Improvement Organization (QIO) review of an individual’s request to continue Medicare-covered services (e.g., SNF stay) and any related claims/requests to pay for continued coverage based on such QIO decision
reasoning: This specifies types of reviews and related claims to be excluded from data submission to CMS.

requirement: A service only covered under the plan’s Medicaid benefits and never covered by Medicare and not covered by the MA plan as a supplemental Medicare benefit (such as Medicaid home-and community-based long-term services and supports)
reasoning: This specifies services covered solely by Medicaid (and not Medicare/supplemental MA) to be excluded from data submission to CMS.

requirement: Plan decisions regarding a request to vacate a dismissal
reasoning: This specifies a particular type of plan decision to be excluded from data submission to CMS.
